title: they want to use it, but they need you to set it up" | Felix Plapperer
full_text:
"Enterprises buying AI are like your grandma getting an iPhone: they want to use it, but they need you to set it up"
"Enterprises buying AI are like your grandma getting an iPhone: they want to use it, but they need you to set it up"
AI-first startups often dream of plug-and-play agents that “just work” — but replacing systems of record like Salesforce, Workday & Co. isn’t a wrapper game.
Tech ventures who win are much rather:
→ Building complicated AI apps
→ Embracing implementation-heavy GTM
→ Hiring forward-deployed teams to *make it work* inside messy enterprise environments
Think PLG is dead? Not quite. But this AI platform shift is different.
🚨 Software isn’t just helping workers anymore. It is the worker.
And that means founders need to think less like app builders and more like system-of-work architects. That takes integration, context, and yes — services.
So forget margins (early on). Own the ingestion layer. Capture the workflow. The moats will follow.
💥 I love seeing AI startups doing just that. Build the messy stuff. That’s where enduring value lives.
#AI #EnterpriseSoftware #PLG #Startups #VentureCapital #BOOOM 💥
